content: NOTES TO ACCOMPANY MAY 2004 35th UPDATE – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter NOTES TO ACCOMPANY MAY 2004 35th UPDATE Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » NOTES TO ACCOMPANY MAY 2004 35th UPDATE Menu ≡ NOTES TO ACCOMPANY MAY 2004 35th UPDATE Introduction The 35 th Update of the Civil Procedure Rules introduces a number of notable changes to procedures including the following: A new Part 65 and accompanying Practice Direction is inserted containing rules about proceedings under various statutes relating to anti-social behaviour and harassment. Its coming into force date is set at 30 June 2004 to coincide with most of the provisions of the Anti-Social Behaviour Act 2003. Part 56 (landlord and tenant claims) is amended as a result of changes to the substantive law on renewal and termination of business tenancies in Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 made in the Regulatory Reform (Business Tenancies) (England and Wales) Order 2003. A new section is inserted into Part 45 providing for fixed recoverable success fees to apply in all road traffic accident disputes where the claimant is funded by a conditional fee agreement. A new rule 5.4A is inserted which will give the Attorney General the right to search for, inspect and take a copy of any documents within a court file for the purpose of an application under s.42 of the Supreme Court Act 1981 or s.33 of the Employment Tribunals Act 1996 to restrict vexatious proceedings. Amendments are made to the Practice Direction on EU Competition Law to extend certain of its provisions to claims raising domestic competition law issues relating to the application of Chapter I or II of Part I of the Competition Act 1998. Corresponding provisions are also inserted in Practice Direction 52 applying to appeals to the Court of Appeal which raise competition law issues. Amendments are made to the Practice Direction on Applications for a warrant under the Competition Act 1998 in consequence of recent amendments to the Act and other related legislation. A number of changes to Practice Direction 52 have been made including new provisions for appeal bundles, skeleton arguments and respondent’s documentation. Also included in PD 52 are a number of provisions formerly included in practice notes and other non-CPR Practice Directions such as those dealing with the handing down of judgments and the short warned list in the Court of Appeal. The Insolvency Practice Direction has been amended to include provisions about bankruptcy restriction orders. A full list of the changes is set out below: Rules Part 5 New rule 5.4A inserted after rule 5.4 Part 6 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK In rule 6.10 new wording ‘state the details set out in the following table’ substituted for existing wording. Part 13 Rule 13.5 is revoked Part 30 In rule 30.8 new wording substituted for existing wording. Part 34 In rule 34.16 ‘another Regulation State’ substituted for ‘Regulation State’. In rule 34.23 ‘who is in another Regulation State’ substituted for words ‘who is’ to the end. Part 42 In rule 42.2(5) sub-paragraph (a) new wording substituted for existing wording. Part 45 New Section III ‘Fixed Percentage Increase In road Traffic Accident Claims’ inserted after Section II. Part 55 In rule 55.1 existing sub-paragraph (d) replaced by new sub-paragraphs (d) to (g). New cross-reference inserted after paragraph 55.2 rule 1. After rule 55.9 (d) new sub-paragraph (e) inserted . In rule 55.12 existing text numbered paragraph (1), new paragraph (2) added. Part 56 In rule 56.2 paragraph 1 ‘paragraphs (2) or (4) apply’ amended to ‘paragraph 2 applies’. Paragraph 4 is revoked. In rule 56.3 existing text replaced by new text. Part 57 In rule 57.16 ‘the time’ is replaced by ‘subject to paragraph (4A) the time’. New paragraph (4A) inserted. Part 65 New Part 65 ‘Proceedings Relating to Anti-Social Behaviour and Harassment’ inserted. Part 69 In rule 69.10 existing text is renumbered as paragraph (1). New paragraph (2) inserted. Schedules Schedule 1 RSC Order 94 Rule 16 is revoked. RSC Order 98 Revoked CCR Order 49 Rule 6B is revoked Practice Directions Practice Direction 2B In paragraph 5.1 existing sub-paragraph (j) is replaced by new text. New heading to paragraph 8 replaces existing heading. In paragraph 8.1 new sub-paragraph (d) inserted. New paragraph 8.1A inserted. In paragraph 8.3 the words ‘section 3 of the Protection from Harassment Act’ are deleted. In paragraph 11.1 sub-paragraph (a)(ii) new wording ‘or for the termination of a tenancy under section 29(2)’ inserted after ‘section 24’. In sub-paragraph (b) after ‘land’ new wording inserted. In sub-paragraph (d) ‘the consent of the parties and’ is deleted. Practice Direction 4 This Practice Direction has been amended to reflect the form changes set out below. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 6B In paragraph 1.2 existing text between ‘tenancies’ and ‘hear the claim’ replaced by new text. In paragraph 1.3 existing text between words ‘agreement’ and ‘applies’ replaced by new text. New paragraph 1.3D inserted after paragraph 1.3C. In paragraph 1.4 the words ‘or 1.3C’ are replaced by ‘, 1.3C or 1.3D’ In paragraph 5.2 sub-paragraph (8) existing text replaced by new text. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 7C In sub-paragraph (4) ‘8.3’ replaced by ‘7.3’. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 8 In paragraph 4.3 words ‘such as a consent application under section 38 of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954’ deleted. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 8B In section B.1 paragraph (2) deleted. In Table 2 the entries for RSC O.98 r.2(1), CCR O.49 r.5(1), CCR O.49 r.6B(1) and CCR O.49 r.10(3) are deleted. Practice Direction 12 In paragraph 4.3(2) existing sub-paragraph (c) replaced by new text. Practice Direction 18 New paragraph 1.7 inserted. Practice Direction 22 In paragraph 1.1 new sub-paragraph (6) inserted. Practice Direction 26 In paragraph 8.1, sub-paragraph (1)(c) ‘those for possession’ replaced by new wording. In paragraph 10.1 ‘The following paragraphs do not apply’ replaced by ‘Paragraph 10.2 does not apply’. In sub-paragraph (1) ‘where the defendant has filed a defence’ replaced by new wording. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 26B A new Practice Direction is included providing for a pilot mediation scheme to operate at the Central London Civil Justice Centre. Practice Direction 40B In paragraph 9.1 ‘the penal notice’ replaced by ‘a penal notice’. Practice Direction 45 In paragraph 25A.6 ‘The areas referred to in rule 45.9(2) are’ is replaced by ‘The area referred to in rule 45.9(2) consists of’ Practice Direction 52 Existing Section 1 replaced by new Section 1. In the table at paragraph 20.1 headed ‘Appeals to the Court of Appeal’ ‘Articles 81 and 82 of the EC Treaty and Chapters I and II of Part I of the Competition Act 1998 21.10A’ inserted as the first entry. After paragraph 21.10A new paragraph 21.10A inserted. Practice Direction 55 New paragraph 1.9 inserted. In paragraph 2.2 ‘2.3 and 2.4’ are replaced by ‘2.3, 2.4 and 2.4A’ New paragraph 2.4A inserted. New paragraph 2.7 inserted. Paragraph 6.1 revoked. Practice Direction 56 At the beginning of paragraph 2.1 ‘The’; replaced by ‘Subject to paragraph 2.1A, the’ New paragraph 2.1A inserted. In paragraph ‘or rule 56.2(4)’ deleted. Existing paragraphs 3.1 to 3.8 are replaced by new paragraphs 3.1 to 3.19 ‘Claims for a new tenancy under section 24 and termination of a tenancy under section 29(2) of the 1954 Act’. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 63 In paragraph 8.1 at the end of sub-paragraph (4) ‘and’ is omitted. New sub-paragraph (6) inserted. Practice Direction 65 New Practice Direction ‘Anti-Social Behaviour and Harassment’ inserted to supplement new Part 65. Practice Direction 70 Paragraph 3 renumbered as paragraph 3.1. ‘sheriff’s return’ replaced by ‘relevant enforcement officer’s return’. New paragraph 3.2 inserted. In paragraph 7.2 ‘Sheriff’s Office’ replaced by ‘relevant enforcement officer’. Other Practice Directions CCR Order 49, Housing Act 1996: Injunction Rule 6B revoked. Anti-social Behaviour (orders under section 1B(4) of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998) Revoked. Insolvency Proceedings New section ‘16A Bankruptcy restrictions orders’ inserted after paragraph 16.8. Competition Law – Claims Relating to the Application of Articles 81 and 82 of the EC Treaty Existing Practice Direction replaced by new Practice Direction. Application for a Warrant under the Competition Act 1998 Existing practice Direction and annexes replaced. Forms Existing forms have been amended as follows: N5 Claim form for possession of property N7 Notes for defendant (mortgaged residential property) N7A Notes for defendant (rented residential property) N7B Notes for defendant (forfeiture of the lease) N11R Defence form (rented residential premises) N119 Particulars of claim for possession (rented residential premises) N119A Notes for guidance on completing particulars of claim N215 Certificate of Service N293A Combined Certificate of Judgment & Request for Writ of Fieri Facias N322H Request to register a High Court Judgment New forms are introduced as follows: N6 Claim form for demotion of tenancy N7D Notes for defendant – demotion claim N11D Defence form (demotion of tenancy) N16A Application for Injunction (general form) N122 Particulars of claim for demotion of tenancy N206D Notice of issue (demotion claim) Coming into force All changes come into force on 30 June with the following exceptions: 1 st June 2004 Rule 5.4A Rule 30.8 Rule 34.16 (1) Rule 34.23 Rule 42.2 Part 45 Section III Rule 56.2 Rule 56.3 PD 2B (amendments to paragraphs 5.1(j) and 11(1)(a)(ii) only) PD 8 PD 45 PD 52 (amendment to paragraph 20.1 and insertion of paragraph 21.10A only) PD 56 PD 70 Practice Direction – Insolvency Proceedings Practice Direction – Competition Law – Claims relating to the application of Articles 81 and 82 of the EC Treaty and Chapters I and II of Part I of the Competition Act 1998 Practice Direction – Application for a warrant under the Competition Act 1998 1 st April Forms N293A N 215 N322H N7 N7B PD26B Transitional provisions 1. –i) In the circumstances where article 29(1) or (4) of the Regulatory Reform (Business Tenancies) (England and Wales) Order 2003 1 applies – the amendments to Part 56 made by rules 15 and 16 of these Rules shall not apply; and Part 56 shall continue to apply on and after 1 st June 2004 as if those amendments had not been made. (2) Where an application for an injunction under Chapter III of Part V of the Housing Act 1996 2 has been issued before 30 th June 2004 – Section I of Part 65 shall not apply in relation to that application; and CCR Order 49, rule 6B shall continue to apply on and after 30th June 2004 as if it had not been revoked. Footnotes 1. S.I. 2003/3096. 2. 1996 c.52.
